page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features 20 vCPUs mapped to 20 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.2 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the server exhibits average multi-core performance but poor single-core performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng results. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks falls into the average tier, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 69.81 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 29.73 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations perform well, with bzip3 decompression scoring in the strong tier at 182.69 GB/sec. Geekbench and Passmark testing consistently place the server in the average category across compiling, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations. Small-model LLM inference shows strong performance, while medium and large model processing remains average.

Given its shared CPU allocation and lack of bundled local storage, this server offers a resource-dense configuration for workloads that require high parallelization but can tolerate shared CPU resources. It is suitable for multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and lightweight LLM inference. However, the poor single-core performance and shared CPU model make it less suitable for latency-sensitive or single-threaded primary databases.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is powered by 20 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. The instance does not include local storage, GPUs, or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench scores landing in the middle tier, though single-core performance is poor. Notably, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM inference show strong performance. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized, non-latency-sensitive workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight text processing.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-64GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 20 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance suitable for parallelized workloads.

tagline: A shared-core virtual server with twenty vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory with 3.2 GB per core
